page: Microsoft Azure E96-24ads_v5 is an EADSv5-family virtual machine configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at 3.525 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The hardware allocation provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 28.0 GB per core. It includes 3686 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory performance is a notable highlight, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, though memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks remain in the average tier. In database and application workloads, the server achieves average results for Redis SET operations and PassMark database operations. For language model tasks, text generation with a small 135M model is strong, whereas prompt processing for a large 70B model is poor.

The E96-24ads_v5 offers a specialized resource density profile, pairing a constrained vCPU count with a massive memory footprint and substantial local storage. This design provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require high memory capacity and local disk space without needing the licensing costs or processing power of a full 96-vCPU instance. It is suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and data caching workloads that benefit from large RAM allocations and local NVMe storage.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-24ads_v5 is an EADSv5-family virtual machine featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM, delivering a high memory-to-core ratio of 28.0 GB. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz, the server includes 3686 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and a strong PassMark memory score, while multi-core CPU, database operations, and memory bandwidth metrics generally align with average tiers. The server lacks GPU accelerators. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not require high vCPU counts, making it suitable for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-24ads_v5 is an AMD EPYC-powered virtual machine featuring 24 vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and 3686 GB of local storage, delivering strong single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96-24ads_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 24 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3686 GB local storage, running on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
672.0 GB of system memory providing 28.0 GB per core
3686 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark performance
Optimized for memory-intensive databases and caching workloads without vCPU overhead
